html:
The Marble Suit is a wearable Armor, crafted in the Fight Tab. It requires 6 marble and 2 Ropes to craft and an Alchemy Engine to prototype.

It has 1050 durability and absorbs 95% of physical damage, and will also reduce the player's speed by 30% when equipped. Not considering its durability, the marble armor effectively adds 2850 hp to the average survivor.

Contents

  • 1 Tips
  • 2  Trivia
  • 3 Assets
  • 4 Gallery

Tips

  • Because of this armor's speed penalty, it is better suited for tanking damage rather than kiting an opponent. This makes it best on mighty Wolfgang who, thanks to his damage bonus, can kill enemies quickly reducing the damage the armor takes and denying the movement speed penalty when Mighty
  • This armor's high protection makes it a good tanking armor on characters with low max health, such as Maxwell, allowing them to heal less often when tanking a boss. This is especially true in Don't Starve Together where armor absorption does not stack.
  • Due to the speed penalty, it isn't practical to wear this armor all the time. Players can switch to it when they want to tank in combat. Alternatively, a Walking Cane can be equipped along with the Marble Suit, negating most of the speed penalty. However, Walking Cane is not a decent weapon for most encounters. Experienced players can mostly overcome this problem by switching to a weapon before they attack, and switching back to Walking Cane before they dodge. Or alternatively, you can take off the suit when trying to avoid an attack and put it back on after you avoid an attack, but this is a fairly risky move which can require practice.
  • In Don't Starve Together, the decreased durability can leave players deterred from the chestplate. If the player desires to save on Marble while still regularly using this powerful chestplate, wearing armor that takes up the head slot is the best course of action. Football Helmets have 80% protection. By wearing the two pieces of armor, the Marble Suit will only absorb 51.6% of all incoming damage (and 54.3% of all damage absorbed by armor). Doing so, the relatively small 735 durability is effectively increased to 1,353 - almost double.
  • In Don't Starve Together, Wolfgang is unaffected by the speed penalty of this item while in his Mighty form. This makes it an ideal armor due to its high protection with virtually no drawback.

 Trivia

  • The Marble Suit was added in the The End Is Nigh update.
  • As of the It's Not A Rock! update, the Marble Suit is renewable, since Marble can be obtained from Earthquakes in Caves. It can also be mined from Marble Shrubs grown from Marble Beans in Don't Starve Together.
  • A character wearing a Marble Suit makes a small clanking noise when walking.
  • In the May 28, 2020 patch for Don't Starve Together, the cost of the Marble Suit was cut in half to 6 marble and 2 rope.

Assets

  • Dropped
  • Equipped

Gallery

  • Wilson wearing a Marble Suit.
  • Willow wearing a Marble Suit.
  • Wolfgang wearing a Marble Suit.
  • Wendy wearing a Marble Suit.
  • WX-78 wearing a Marble Suit.
  • Wickerbottom wearing a Marble Suit.
  • Woodie wearing a Marble Suit.
  • Wes wearing a Marble Suit.
  • Maxwell wearing a Marble Suit.
  • Wigfrid wearing a Marble Suit.
  • Webber wearing a Marble Suit.
  • Walani wearing a Marble Suit.
  • Warly wearing a Marble Suit.
  • Wilbur wearing a Marble Suit.
  • Woodlegs wearing a Marble Suit.
  • Wilba wearing a Marble Suit.
  • Wormwood wearing a Marble Suit.
  • Wheeler wearing a Marble Suit.
  • Winona wearing a Marble Suit.
  • Wortox wearing a Marble Suit.
  • Wurt wearing a Marble Suit.
  • Marble Suit as seen in the poster for the The End is Nigh update.
Tools and WeaponsCraftable Items

"armormarble"
“This looks really heavy.”
–Wilson
“If I have to fight I might as well be protected.”
–Willow
“I tie rocks to muscles.”
–Wolfgang
“Could stop the daggers that stab at mine heart.”
–Wendy
“EXTRA HEAVY DUTY CASING.”
–WX-78
“An interesting choice of materials.”
–Wickerbottom
“It's hard to swing an axe while wearing this.”
–Woodie
“That really isn't my style.”
–Maxwell
“Its weight increases when worn, but not when carried. Fascinating!”
–Wagstaff
“Nearly impenetrable!”
–Wigfrid
“Marbelous protection!”
–Webber
“That's just overkill.”
–Walani
“Weighs a ton.”
–Warly
“This'd sink like a stone in th'sea.”
–Woodlegs
“'TIS ARMOR MADE OF ROCK BITS”
–Wilba
“Heavy”
–Wormwood
“It's like wearing a tank. Like driving a tank too.”
–Wheeler
“Protects your inner workings.”
–Winona
“Stone cold!”
–Wortox
“It heavyyyy...”
–Wurt
“Will I be able to walk in that?”
–Walter
“Well it's definitely sturdy, but why does it have to be so heavy?!”
–Wanda
The Marble Suit is a wearable Armor, crafted in the Fight Tab. It requires 6 marble and 2 Ropes to craft and an Alchemy Engine to prototype.
It has 1050 durability and absorbs 95% of physical damage, and will also reduce the player's speed by 30% when equipped. Not considering its durability, the marble armor effectively adds 2850 hp to the average survivor.
Contents
1 Tips
2  Trivia
3 Assets
4 Gallery
Tips[edit | edit source]
Because of this armor's speed penalty, it is better suited for tanking damage rather than kiting an opponent. This makes it best on mighty Wolfgang who, thanks to his damage bonus, can kill enemies quickly reducing the damage the armor takes and denying the movement speed penalty when Mighty
This armor's high protection makes it a good tanking armor on characters with low max health, such as Maxwell, allowing them to heal less often when tanking a boss. This is especially true in Don't Starve Together where armor absorption does not stack.
Due to the speed penalty, it isn't practical to wear this armor all the time. Players can switch to it when they want to tank in combat. Alternatively, a Walking Cane can be equipped along with the Marble Suit, negating most of the speed penalty. However, Walking Cane is not a decent weapon for most encounters. Experienced players can mostly overcome this problem by switching to a weapon before they attack, and switching back to Walking Cane before they dodge. Or alternatively, you can take off the suit when trying to avoid an attack and put it back on after you avoid an attack, but this is a fairly risky move which can require practice.
In Don't Starve Together, the decreased durability can leave players deterred from the chestplate. If the player desires to save on Marble while still regularly using this powerful chestplate, wearing armor that takes up the head slot is the best course of action. Football Helmets have 80% protection. By wearing the two pieces of armor, the Marble Suit will only absorb 51.6% of all incoming damage (and 54.3% of all damage absorbed by armor). Doing so, the relatively small 735 durability is effectively increased to 1,353 - almost double.
In Don't Starve Together, Wolfgang is unaffected by the speed penalty of this item while in his Mighty form. This makes it an ideal armor due to its high protection with virtually no drawback.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
The Marble Suit was added in the The End Is Nigh update.
As of the It's Not A Rock! update, the Marble Suit is renewable, since Marble can be obtained from Earthquakes in Caves. It can also be mined from Marble Shrubs grown from Marble Beans in Don't Starve Together.
A character wearing a Marble Suit makes a small clanking noise when walking.
In the May 28, 2020 patch for Don't Starve Together, the cost of the Marble Suit was cut in half to 6 marble and 2 rope.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Bộ Đồ Cẩm Thạch là một loại Áo Giáp có thể đeo được, được chế tạo trong Thẻ Chiến đấu. Nó cần 6 viên bi và 2 Dây thừng để chế tạo và Máy luyện kim để tạo nguyên mẫu.

Like driving a tank too. |winona = Protects your inner workings. |wortox = Stone cold! |wurt = It heavyyyy... |walter = Will I be able to walk in that? |wanda = Well it's definitely sturdy, but why does it have to be so heavy?! |choose = wx78}} The '''Marble Suit''' is a wearable [[Armor]], crafted in the [[Fight Tab]]. It requires 6 marble and 2 Ropes to craft and an [[Alchemy Engine]] to prototype. It has 1050 durability and absorbs 95% of physical damage, and will also reduce the player's speed by 30% when equipped. Not considering its durability, the marble armor effectively adds 2850 hp to the average survivor. == {{Pic32|Prototype}} Tips== * Because of this armor's speed penalty, it is better suited for tanking damage rather than kiting an opponent. This makes it best on mighty [[Wolfgang]] who, thanks to his damage bonus, can kill enemies quickly reducing the damage the armor takes and denying the movement speed penalty when Mighty * This armor's high protection makes it a good tanking armor on characters with low max health, such as [[Maxwell/Character|Maxwell]], allowing them to heal less often when tanking a boss. This is especially true in [[Don't Starve Together]] where armor absorption does not stack. * Due to the speed penalty, it isn't practical to wear this armor all the time. Players can switch to it when they want to tank in combat. Alternatively, a [[Walking Cane]] can be equipped along with the Marble Suit, negating most of the speed penalty. However, Walking Cane is not a decent weapon for most encounters. Experienced players can mostly overcome this problem by switching to a weapon before they attack, and switching back to Walking Cane before they dodge. Or alternatively, you can take off the suit when trying to avoid an attack and put it back on after you avoid an attack, but this is a fairly risky move which can require practice. * In Don't Starve Together, the decreased durability can leave players deterred from the chestplate. If the player desires to save on Marble while still regularly using this powerful chestplate, wearing armor that takes up the head slot is the best course of action. [[Football Helmet|Football Helmets]] have 80% protection. By wearing the two pieces of armor, the Marble Suit will only absorb 51.6% of all incoming damage (and 54.3% of all damage absorbed by armor). Doing so, the relatively small 735 durability is effectively increased to 1,353 - almost double. * In Don't Starve Together, [[Wolfgang]] is unaffected by the speed penalty of this item while in his Mighty form. This makes it an ideal armor due to its high protection with virtually no drawback. == {{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * The Marble Suit was added in the ''[[Version History#April 9, 2013 - The End is Nigh|The End Is Nigh]]'' update. * As of the ''[[New Features#June 11.2C 2013 - It.27s not a rock.21|It's Not A Rock!]]'' update, the Marble Suit is renewable, since Marble can be obtained from [[Earthquakes]] in [[Caves]]. It can also be mined from Marble Shrubs grown from [[Marble Bean]]s in ''[[Don't Starve Together]]''. * A character wearing a Marble Suit makes a small clanking noise when walking. *In the May 28, 2020 patch for ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'', the cost of the Marble Suit was cut in half to 6 marble and 2 rope. == {{Pic32|Research Notes}} Assets == Marble Suit Dropped.png|Dropped Marble Suit Equipped.png|Equipped == {{Pic32|Blueprint}} Gallery == Marble Suit Wilson.png|[[Wilson]] wearing a Marble Suit.
